acceptanceCriteria: the CODEOWNERS finding is renamed to codeowners-sampled-coverage, its satisfied detail states it is a sampled early warning and not proof of full glob coverage, and the report field is renamed sampledPaths

the GitHub reader enforces a 10 second timeout, rejects redirects, treats unparseable or oversized (>1MB) response bodies as unverifiable rather than failed, and sends only GET requests to https://api.github.com with the token in the authorization header only

docs/threat-model.md documents the GitHub API as a trust boundary covering transmitted data, allowed host, token precedence, redirect refusal, timeout, response size limit, error concealment, and the GET-only mechanism
README documents the recommended execution order (edit everything including dist, then verify, then review, then one commit) and the write-once artifact behavior requiring a new task ID for revisions
unit tests cover the reader hardening (host, method, headers, oversize and unparseable bodies) and the renamed sampled finding
npm run test:all passes and the verification covers the full pull-request change set
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
